nodes.name: Executable Specification
bullets: Keep harness control flow in a repo-owned artifact instead of burying it in controller code.
Branches, loops, parallelism, retries, and handoffs should be inspectable and diffable as product logic.
For PlotLot, the zoning/site-feasibility workflow should be editable as a stage graph: intake -> retrieve -> extract -> verify -> calculate -> report -> review.

nodes.name: Step-Bounded Context
bullets: Context should be granted per step, not inherited by default from one ever-growing transcript.
Fresh-task vs persistent-step semantics let the harness decide when to isolate context and when to preserve it.
For PlotLot, ordinance extraction should see only the parcel facts and cited ordinance sections needed for that extraction lane.

nodes.name: Workflow Module Interface
bullets: Treat skills, tools, and subagents as workflow modules with named parameters and typed return values.
A reusable module boundary is the unit of delegation, testing, and replacement.
PlotLot modules should include parcel facts, zoning retrieval, dimensional extraction, conflict resolution, deterministic calculation, and evidence review.

nodes.name: Replayable Trajectory
bullets: Checkpoint after each stage with step id, context snapshot, metrics, tool outputs, and sandbox/workspace state.
Selective replay lets you hold upstream evidence constant while changing a downstream prompt or verifier.
For PlotLot, replay should avoid redoing GIS and ordinance fetches when only extraction, report, or review logic changes.

nodes.name: Workflow Verification
bullets: Define preconditions and postconditions at the workflow-step level so runs are testable as trajectories, not just final answers.
Use both static checks over the plan and dynamic checks over the realized execution trace.
For PlotLot, verification means every extracted zoning number is unit-normalized, source-cited, and reproducible by deterministic calculators.

nodes.name: Versioned Agent Snapshot
bullets: Treat every optimizer or harness change as a discrete, diffable version so improvements can be replayed, compared, and rolled back.
Versioning should capture the full eval context, not just code: prompts, dataset slice, and configuration also matter.
For PlotLot, every site-feasibility eval should record git commit, prompt versions, dataset path, and the exact thresholds used to judge the run.

nodes.name: Budget-Gated Evaluator
bullets: Evaluation is a governed resource; the harness should explicitly enforce limits instead of trusting the optimizer to self-budget.
Budgets make comparisons fair because no optimizer gets silent extra search or compute.
For PlotLot, offline site-feasibility evals should log sample budgets now and later expand to tool, runtime, and token budgets.

nodes.name: Structured Observation Interface
bullets: Expose per-sample inputs, outputs, intermediate traces, errors, and scores through a consistent interface across scaffolds.
Fair optimizer comparison depends on standardizing what evidence is visible, not just standardizing the final metric.
For PlotLot, parcel facts, ordinance citations, calculator outputs, and failure labels should be stored in one comparable observation schema.

nodes.name: Agent-Supervised Tool Adaptation
bullets: Freeze the backbone and improve the surrounding searchers, memory writers, planners, and reviewers under its supervision.
Narrow procedural specialists are often more data-efficient and less forgetful than retraining one monolithic agent.
For PlotLot, ordinance retrieval, extraction, evidence review, and memory curation should evolve as peripheral modules around a stable orchestrator.

nodes.name: Adaptation Signal Design
bullets: Separate dense causal execution signals from sparse holistic utility signals; they answer different questions.
Use execution-grounded metrics to debug tool mechanics and end-to-end metrics to judge final analyst value.
For PlotLot, citation resolution, parser correctness, and calculator reproducibility are execution signals; report usefulness and analyst acceptance are holistic signals.

nodes.name: Graduated Subagent
bullets: A specialist can be learned under A1/A2-style adaptation, then frozen and redeployed as a reusable T1 tool.
Graduation turns fragile prompt behavior into a stable interface with measurable responsibilities.
For PlotLot, stable ordinance search, extraction, or review specialists should be promoted into reusable modules callable by the main harness.

nodes.name: Explicit-to-Implicit Translation
bullets: Convert directly observed artifacts into model-legible signals before the agent starts searching or planning.
Keep static translation tools outside the active agent loop; they reduce ambiguity without spending tool budget.
For PlotLot, translate parcel/zoning pages, ordinance PDFs, tables, and map legends into normalized jurisdiction, code, and evidence candidates before LLM synthesis.

nodes.name: Dynamic Reference Expansion
bullets: When a static registry is incomplete, the harness should actively discover additional references during execution.
Reference expansion should be budgeted, queryable, and evidence-producing rather than free-form browsing.
For PlotLot, discover official parcel, zoning, and ordinance hosts from jurisdiction clues instead of assuming complete prior coverage.

nodes.name: Entity-to-Authority Verification
bullets: Predict the governing entity, then verify that the source boundary is official before trusting downstream claims.
Authority verification should absorb domain variants and mirrors while still distinguishing official hosts from lookalikes.
For PlotLot, after predicting city/county/ordinance authority, verify official municipal, county, ArcGIS, or Municode domains before accepting zoning facts.

nodes.name: Budgeted Tool Loop
bullets: Dynamic discovery improves accuracy, but the harness must explicitly limit retrieval rounds, latency, and spend.
Tool loops should expose cost/benefit tradeoffs and stop conditions rather than silently accumulating work.
For PlotLot, ordinance retrieval and evidence resolution should run under explicit budgets for tool calls, tokens, and wall-clock time.

nodes.name: Skill Handbook
bullets: Keep routing knowledge in an explicit artifact containing mode-selection insights, a skill registry, and per-agent profiles.
The handbook is the reusable layer between high-level workflow stages and concrete model/tool choices.
For PlotLot, the handbook should encode when to retrieve, extract, calculate, review, or report, plus the skills each lane requires.

nodes.name: Competence-Cost Agent Profile
bullets: Route agents using skill-conditioned competence estimates and mode-specific cost, not global model reputation.
A profile should accumulate success/failure evidence, routing caveats, and average cost per operational mode.
For PlotLot, specialist lanes like ordinance retrieval, table extraction, and citation-backed synthesis should each earn their own profile statistics.

nodes.name: Contrastive Skill Discovery
bullets: Learn new skills by contrasting successful and failed trajectories at the same mode to isolate the missing capability.
The raw material is execution traces, not hand-authored taxonomies or expensive RL updates.
For PlotLot, compare cited vs uncited retrievals, correct vs incorrect zoning extractions, and accepted vs rejected reports to discover reusable specialist skills.

nodes.name: Granularity-Aware Handbook Selection
bullets: More skills are not always better; keep only the level of detail the orchestrator can reliably recognize and use.
Select handbook subsets on a Pareto frontier of task success versus execution cost.
For PlotLot, start with coarse skill buckets and split them only when eval traces show that the extra distinction improves routing quality.

nodes.name: Context Refresh + Notes Recall
bullets: When long trajectories approach context limits, clear stale dialogue and reconstruct working state from durable notes instead of dragging the whole transcript forward.
Notes are not a convenience log; they are the compression boundary that preserves progress across context resets.
For PlotLot, refresh long site-feasibility runs from the evidence ledger, jurisdiction summary, open questions, and calculator state rather than raw chat history.

nodes.name: Failure Manifestation Catalog
bullets: Separate root causes from visible trajectory failures so evals can diagnose why a run failed, not just that it failed.
Useful manifestations include repetitive looping, premature convergence, misaligned tool usage, memory issues, uncontrolled experiments, error propagation, and environment mis-modeling.
For PlotLot, failed analyses should be labeled by manifestation so we can distinguish bad retrieval, weak planning, stale memory, and premature report commit.

nodes.name: File-as-Bus Workspace
bullets: Use a shared workspace of durable artifacts as the coordination substrate instead of relying on lossy conversational handoffs.
Separate artifact regions by role so analyses, runnable code, plans, and logs persist independently and can be re-read by later agents.

For PlotLot, each site-feasibility case should preserve parcel facts, authority resolution, ordinance evidence, extraction outputs, calculator state, review notes, and final reports as first-class artifacts.

nodes.name: Progressive Disclosure Workspace Map
bullets: Give the orchestrator a compact map/index of the workspace so control decisions stay small while detailed state remains available on demand.
This realizes thin control over thick state: summaries and maps guide stage control, specialists drill into deeper artifacts only when needed.
For PlotLot, the active controller should carry case summary, open questions, artifact locations, and evidence gaps instead of hauling full transcripts and raw ordinance text forward.

nodes.name: Exploration-vs-Exploitation Error Ledger
bullets: Score trajectories by distinguishing exploration failures from exploitation failures instead of collapsing everything into final task success.
In partially observed tasks, low exploration error can be far more predictive of success than low exploitation error, so discovery quality deserves its own metric lane.
For PlotLot, traces should tell us whether a bad analysis came from missing the governing source or from synthesizing the wrong conclusion after the evidence was already available.

nodes.name: Frontier + Activatable-State Summary
bullets: Externalize visited space, reachable frontier, discovered entities, prerequisites, and ready-to-act states into an explicit memory summary.
A lightweight state summary can materially improve behavior by letting the model exploit discovered structure without depending on fragile conversational recall.

For PlotLot, the summary should track verified official hosts, remaining source frontier, discovered ordinance sections, unresolved dimensional questions, and claims whose prerequisites are complete enough for calculation or reporting.

overviewConnections.title: AgentSPEX (2604.13346v1) synthesis
bullets: Connects [[Harness Runtime]] to [[Executable Specification]]: the harness becomes an interpreter/executor for a repo-owned workflow artifact.
Connects [[Context Broker]] to [[Step-Bounded Context]]: task-vs-step semantics make context scope an explicit engineering choice.
Connects [[Evidence Ledger]] to [[Workflow Verification]] and [[Replayable Trajectory]]: the same saved artifacts that support replay also support post-hoc audit and deterministic recomputation.
For PlotLot, this paper sharpens the target architecture for a land-use/site-feasibility harness: modular workflow stages, explicit evidence gates, and replayable vertical runs.

overviewConnections.title: VeRO (2602.22480) synthesis
bullets: Connects [[Harness Runtime]] to [[Versioned Agent Snapshot]]: every quality run should be attributable to a concrete, replayable harness state instead of a fuzzy latest configuration.
Connects [[Workflow Verification]] to [[Budget-Gated Evaluator]]: evaluation budgets are part of the contract, not an afterthought outside the harness.
Connects [[Evidence Ledger]] and [[Replayable Trajectory]] to [[Structured Observation Interface]]: the same saved case artifacts should feed both post-hoc analysis and future optimizer loops.
For PlotLot, this paper pushes the site-feasibility harness toward versioned eval manifests, deterministic sample budgets, and structured per-case observations.

overviewConnections.title: Adaptation of Agentic AI (2512.16301v3) synthesis
bullets: Connects [[Harness Runtime]] to [[Agent-Supervised Tool Adaptation]]: the stable orchestrator should supervise evolving specialist modules rather than absorb every improvement into one agent policy.
Connects [[Evidence Ledger]] to [[Adaptation Signal Design]]: evidence packets and deterministic calculators let PlotLot manufacture A1-style execution signals inside a broader T2 workflow.
Connects [[Workflow Module Interface]] to [[Graduated Subagent]]: once a specialist proves reliable, it should be frozen behind a typed interface and reused across runs.
For PlotLot, this paper supports a frozen-core / adaptive-periphery architecture for the land-use/site-feasibility harness.

overviewConnections.title: Automated Phishing Detection using URLs and Webpages (2408.01667v2) synthesis
bullets: Connects [[Context Broker]] to [[Explicit-to-Implicit Translation]]: before context selection, the harness should translate raw artifacts into higher-order signals.
Connects [[Harness Runtime]] to [[Dynamic Reference Expansion]] and [[Budgeted Tool Loop]]: dynamic discovery beats static coverage, but only if retrieval budgets are explicit.
Connects [[Governance Middleware]] and [[Evidence Ledger]] to [[Entity-to-Authority Verification]]: first verify the official source boundary, then accept downstream claims.

For PlotLot, the direct analogue is jurisdiction/source discovery: predict the governing city/county plus ordinance host, verify official domains, then cache trusted references for future site-feasibility runs.

overviewConnections.title: SkillOrchestra (2602.19672v1) synthesis
bullets: Connects [[Harness Runtime]] to [[Skill Handbook]]: routing policy should live in a reusable artifact instead of hiding in prompt folklore or hardcoded model preferences.
Connects [[Evidence Ledger]] and [[Replayable Trajectory]] to [[Contrastive Skill Discovery]]: the same traces used for replay can be mined into capability gaps and reusable specialist skills.
Connects [[Workflow Verification]] to [[Competence-Cost Agent Profile]]: per-skill success, citation coverage, and deterministic agreement become the evidence base for routing decisions.

For PlotLot, this paper suggests a land-use handbook that routes among retrieval, extraction, calculation, and review specialists while explicitly monitoring cost and collapse into one expensive lane.

overviewConnections.title: UltraHorizon (2509.21766) synthesis
bullets: Connects [[Context Broker]] and [[Evidence Ledger]] to [[Context Refresh + Notes Recall]]: long-horizon performance depends on artifact-backed state reconstruction, not ever-growing transcripts.

Connects [[Workflow Verification]] and [[Replayable Trajectory]] to [[Failure Manifestation Catalog]]: traces should be replayable and labelable by premature convergence, looping, memory loss, and uncontrolled experiments.
For PlotLot, this paper argues that site-feasibility is a genuinely long-horizon agent problem and needs explicit context-reset, evidence-threshold, and failure-diagnosis machinery.

overviewConnections.title: AiScientist (2604.13018v1) synthesis
bullets: Connects [[Harness Runtime]] to [[File-as-Bus Workspace]]: durable case artifacts should be the system of record for long-horizon work, not inherited chat context.

Connects [[Context Broker]] to [[Progressive Disclosure Workspace Map]]: control stays compact when the orchestrator navigates the case through summaries and a workspace index instead of raw artifact bulk.

Connects [[Evidence Ledger]] and [[Replayable Trajectory]] to later-round refinement: the real value of saved state appears after the first runnable pass, when the harness needs to diagnose and patch hard cases without restarting.
For PlotLot, this paper supports a parcel-centric artifact bus with specialist lanes for authority discovery, ordinance retrieval, extraction, calculation, and review.

overviewConnections.title: Exploration and Exploitation Errors Are Measurable (2604.13151v1) synthesis
bullets: Connects [[Workflow Verification]] and [[Evidence Ledger]] to [[Exploration-vs-Exploitation Error Ledger]]: site-feasibility runs should be judged by what kind of trajectory mistake happened, not only whether the final answer was right.

Connects [[Context Broker]] to [[Frontier + Activatable-State Summary]]: explicit frontier and ready-state memory helps the harness know what still needs discovery versus what is already safe to exploit.
Connects [[Failure Manifestation Catalog]] to search-vs-synthesis diagnosis: a bad PlotLot run can fail because it never found the decisive source or because it mishandled evidence it already had.
For PlotLot, this paper argues for frontier-aware retrieval, prerequisite-gated claims, and separate metrics for source discovery quality versus downstream synthesis quality.
AgentSPEX is the clearest reviewed paper so far on converting harness logic into an executable, human-editable workflow spec.
Its strongest contribution for PlotLot is not the YAML syntax itself, but the combination of explicit context boundaries, checkpointed execution, and replayable downstream experimentation.
Its main gap relative to other reviewed governance papers is weak policy language; PlotLot should pair this workflow style with stronger approval and least-privilege controls.
This is the clearest reviewed paper so far on where adaptation should live in an agentic system.
For PlotLot, its most important consequence is strategic: freeze the orchestrator, and improve the surrounding retrieval, memory, review, and evidence modules.
Use dense execution signals wherever possible inside the land-use harness, and reserve holistic judging for truly end-to-end synthesis quality.
The paper matters less for phishing per se than for its harness decomposition: static signal translation -> budgeted discovery -> authority verification.
Its most transferable primitive for PlotLot is replacing exhaustive prior registries with on-run reference expansion backed by official-source verification.

Its main warning is operational: dynamic discovery improved accuracy dramatically but paid ~20s/sample and depended on brittle external services, so PlotLot needs caching, fallback routes, and strict retrieval budgets.
This is the clearest reviewed paper so far on replacing end-to-end router training with an explicit, reusable routing knowledge base.
For PlotLot, its biggest architectural consequence is to route by capability lanes backed by trace-derived evidence, not by one static default model per stage.
Its most important warning is granularity: a richer skill taxonomy only helps if the orchestrator can reliably classify active skills and use the distinction to improve cost or accuracy.
UltraHorizon is the clearest newly ingested paper so far on how long-horizon agent failures compound as trajectories, tool calls, and partial observability increase.
Its most transferable primitive for PlotLot is CRNR: reset active context and rebuild from durable notes/evidence instead of trusting one swollen transcript.

Its biggest warning for PlotLot is evaluation realism: more steps alone do not solve hard site-feasibility cases unless the harness also enforces evidence thresholds and controlled hypothesis testing.
VeRO is the clearest newly ingested paper so far on what an optimization-ready eval harness must preserve: versioning, budgets, permissions, reproducibility, and trace visibility.
For PlotLot, its most immediate consequence is operational rather than model-centric: make every site-feasibility quality run attributable to an exact harness state and evidence slice.
Its main warning is that optimizer behavior collapses toward prompt edits unless the harness exposes richer structural signals and compares changes under identical budgets.
AiScientist is the clearest reviewed paper so far on durable state continuity as the backbone of long-horizon engineering work.

Its most transferable PlotLot pattern is a parcel-centric artifact bus: preserve evolving evidence, plans, code/calculator outputs, and review notes so hard cases can be resumed and refined instead of restarted.

Its biggest warning is operational: later-round refinement collapses without saved intermediate state, so transcript-only case handling will underperform exactly where difficult site-feasibility work gets interesting.
This paper is the clearest reviewed source so far on separating discovery failures from synthesis failures in partially observed agent tasks.

For PlotLot, its most practical consequence is to maintain an explicit frontier/ready-state summary so the harness knows which sources still need discovery and which claims are mature enough for downstream calculation or reporting.
Its biggest warning is that semantic intuition is unreliable: domain priors can help some models and hurt others, so PlotLot must gate conclusions on evidence completeness rather than plausibility.
